concerns.analysis: The HTML output is missing several meaningful images from the source PDF. Specifically, the cover page of the source PDF contains a collage of four photographs and the North Carolina State University logo, none of which are present in the HTML output. Furthermore, several charts are incorrectly cropped or missing: Figure 7 on page 15 and Figure 11 on page 19 are missing their actual charts, with the HTML instead containing cropped images of the page footers/blank space. Figures 1, 2, and 9 were
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains a collage of four photos and an NC State University logo. Page 15 contains a bar/line chart for Figure 7.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output completely lacks the tags for the cover photos and logo. For Figure 7, the HTML contains an image with the alt text 'The image displays the footer of a document page', indicating a severe mis-crop.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (38): Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023; 1 | P a g e; Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=38, pages_affected=19/20. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=2. Examples: p8: "LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023"; p9: "LAKE GASTON HYDRILLA TUBER BANK ESTIMATES". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

The source document contains underlined text on Page 1 ("Prepared By:" and "Submitted To:") to indicate distinct sections/labels. In the extraction JSON, this text is only italicized and has lost the original underline emphasis.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
